summary.intent: Address a Codex code review pass on PR #691 (fogodev/ars-ui) for the NavigationMenu component, fix identified issues around controlled prop state management, and get CI green.
summary.outcome: All seven original Codex review threads were resolved with commit 50e6d2b6, a second Codex pass identified four follow-up findings which were also patched and committed as 'fix navigation menu controlled followups', and CI was rerun after a runner failure obscured unit test results.

repo: NavigationMenu uses a Bindable abstraction where controlled props are the public source of truth; internal pending state must wait for parent sync rather than reading raw internal values.
Adapter timestamps must be forwarded through EscapeKey events rather than hard-coded to 0 — keydown helpers in trigger/content require these timestamps.
OpenTimerFired must guard against stale timers by only opening when the fired item matches the currently pending open item.
SetItems must close and clear internal open state when the active key is removed, and also clear pending hover/content state.
SyncControlledValue must record the previous bindable value before applying the new controlled value to preserve motion direction attrs.
SyncProps should not erase a runtime-resolved Direction::Auto — direction sync needs special handling for this case.
The project uses cargo nextest (xci-fast) for fast local test runs and llvm-cov for coverage.

code.finding: Second-pass fixes applied here: Escape focus handling, SetItems registration cleanup, resolved Direction::Auto sync in SyncProps, and open-state reconvergence logic.

workflow: Codex review is triggered by posting '@codex review' as a PR comment via gh cli; it reacts with an eyes emoji while processing.
Review threads can be bulk-resolved via GitHub GraphQL API using the resolveReviewThread mutation with thread node IDs.
CI runner failures can produce a 'failed' job conclusion with no actual test output — rerunning with 'gh run rerun --failed' is the right first response before changing code.
The gh-fix-ci Codex plugin skill at ~/.codex/plugins/cache/openai-curated/github/.../skills/gh-fix-ci/ provides scripted CI inspection tooling.
The project uses 'cargo +nightly fmt --all' for formatting and 'cargo clippy -p ars-components --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings' for lint.

friction: CI unit tests job showed as 'failed' with no test failure output — the job log truncated after dependency setup with the step still marked in-progress, requiring a rerun rather than a code fix.
Multiple polling sleep loops were needed to track Codex review completion and CI status, with no push-based notification available.
GitHub GraphQL thread IDs had to be fetched and resolved individually for each of the seven review threads.
The gh-fix-ci inspect script failed with python and had to be retried with python3.
open_items: Unit Tests job rerun outcome not confirmed in transcript — need to verify final CI green state before merge.
Second Codex review pass results after the follow-up commit are not yet confirmed — another '@codex review' cycle may be needed.
The Direction::Auto runtime resolution edge case in SyncProps may warrant additional integration test coverage beyond the unit regressions added.
